observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries
to take up the understatement of the CIP – Investment Property Buildings,
Government Equity and Grants and Donations accounts to reflect their
correct balances; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the Construction in
Progress accounts are used to account for accumulated cost of projects
that are still in process of construction or development.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries
to take up the reclassification of the Due to GOCCs account pertaining to the
receipts of PhilHealth Konsulta Package Capitation to the Trust
Liability account; and
b. Ensure that programmed projects under the Sixty Percent (60%)
allocation of the capitation reimbursement are implemented for the
essential services, medicines provided and other operating expense to
support delivery of health care in the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries
to take up the net overstatement of the affected Property, Plant and
Equipment accounts amounting to ₱10,694,174.96 and to reflect the
correct balance of the PPE accounts; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the correct recognition of
Depreciation Expenses in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the:
a. MAccO to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the
balances of the RPT Receivables, SET Receivables, Deferred RPT
Income, and Deferred SET Income accounts and ensure that the RPT
Receivables / SET Receivables are established based on the List of the
RPT Payers report of the Municipal Treasurer;
b. Municipal Treasurer and MAccO to reconcile their records annually on
RPT and SET Receivables and make the necessary adjustments or
corrections; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to submit their RPT and SET collections reports to
the MAccO on time to ensure that recording of RPT Income for RPT and
SET are not delayed.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Consider creating a separate bank account solely for the transfer of local
development funds from the GF to the new MDF account, therefore the old
MDF account would only be used for tax remittances;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to examine the above cited differences
and make the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the balances of
the reciprocal accounts; and
c. Monitor the intra-agency transactions and ensure the reconciliation and
elimination of intra-agency accounts in the consolidated financial
statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all
Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality and report the same in
the Report on the Physical Count of PPE to be reconciled with accounting
records; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to
investigate why PPEs that were physically verified were not included in
the accounting records and thereby record the PPEs in the books of the
Municipality if necessary.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize the
semi-expendable properties under the PPE account costing ₱941,514.97 and
to reflect the correct balances of other affected accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Council to update annually the LDRRMF
Investment Plan to include activities to be funded from the unexpended
LDRRMF of the previous years.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Health Officer to submit the supporting schedules
or computation for the individual incentives received by the Physicians,
Admin Staff and Medical/Allied/Other Staff groups as well as the certified
list of eligible health workers and staff; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Health Officer to coordinate with the accounting
office to ensure that the grant of PhilHealth incentives is proper and valid.

We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to
comply with laws, rules and regulations to avoid audit suspensions,
disallowances, and charges and settle the same within the prescribed period
to prevent their accumulation.

1.8. We recommended and Management agreed to exert efforts in locating the
MOA or communicate with the source agencies to determine the proper
disposition of the fund.

1.9. We further recommended and Management agreed to ensure constant
monitoring on the implementation of projects and immediate return of
funds to the source agency, if warranted, to avoid accumulation of
unutilized fund transfers from NGAs.

1.10. Finally, we recommended and Management agreed to require the
Municipal Accountant to draw the following entry to correct the misstated
balances:
Account Title Debit Credit
Due to NGAs 18,178,220.85
Due to GOCCs 18,153,176.85

2.22. We recommended and Management agreed to reconstitute the Inventory
and Disposal Committee composed of members who shall be temporarily
relieved of their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of
the physical inventory taking, ensuring that at least one member shall
come from the Municipal Accounting and Treasury Office.

2.23. We also recommended and Management agreed to require the newly
constituted Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct the physical
count of PPEs including road networks and ensure that a detailed and
complete RPCPPE and RPCRNC to facilitate proper reconciliation of
property records and account balances.

2.24. We further recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
a. Prepare property cards per category of PPE and use the same to update
the RPCPPE and PARs;
b. Prepare and submit the RLRN; and
c. Maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads
and its components.

2.25. Finally, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Update the ledgers and accounting records upon receipt of the
RPCPPE;
b. Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card for the roads
and its components; and
c. Draw the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous
depreciation.

3.9. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Restate the balance of accounts affected by prior period adjustments;
b. Recognize the prior period errors in the Prior Period Adjustment
account; and
c. Disclose the nature and impact of the errors in the NFS.

4.8. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accounting Office to:
a. Transfer the unspent LDRRMF to the Trust Fund books and consider
the following entries:
General Fund books
Prior Period Adjustment 4,415,678.09
Cash Local Treasury 4,415,678.09
Trust Fund books
Cash Local Treasury 4,415,678.09
Trust Liability-DRRM 4,415,678.09
b. Set-up and maintain subsidiary ledgers on the unexpended LDRRMF for
CYs 2019 and 2020 for monitoring of its utilization;
c. Verify the variances between the LDRRMF Utilization Report and NFS
and make the necessary corrections in the balances; and
d. Henceforth, ensure that the amounts reported in the utilization reports
correspond with that in the NFS.

5.4. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a. Acting Municipal Treasurer to segregate from the collection of building
permit fees and other charges the 15 per cent share of the OBO and the
five per cent share of the DPWH to be remitted to the Bureau of
Treasury;
b. Municipal Accountant to record the OBO share under the Trust
Liabilities account and the DPWH share under the Due to NGAs
account; and
c. Municipal Engineer to utilize the 15 per cent share by preparing a
program of work approved by the Municipal Mayor and submitted to
the NBCDO.

5.5. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw
necessary adjusting entries to record the OBO and DPWH share.

6.8. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that reciprocal entries are made and reconciled at
year-end to accurately reflect the balance of the Due to Other Funds and
Due from Other Funds accounts.

7.10. We recommended and Management agreed to require the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of supplies and
materials and prepare the RPCI;
b. Acting Municipal Treasurer to refrain from issuing supplies and
materials upon receipt and summarize the RIS to SSMIs and submit the
SSMIs to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the computation of the
cost of the supplies issued and the ending inventory; and
c. Municipal Accountant to record the expenditure of supplies and
materials based on a fully accomplished SSMIs and to observe the
perpetual inventory method in recording the receipt of supplies and
materials.

8.6. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Review and analyze the records of the accounts with abnormal
balances and reconcile accordingly to arrive at the correct balances;
b. Draw correcting entries to eliminate the abnormal balances; and
c. Conduct proper and regular monitoring and review of all accounts to
detect abnormal balances and ensure that only correct account
balances are forwarded to the financial statements.

9.9. We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC to: (a)
ensure that all procurements are in the approved APP; (b) comply with the
prescribed APP format; (c) instruct all end-user to submit their respective
PPMP; and (d) submit the approved APP to the GPPB and audit team.

10.8. We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC to:
a. Undertake infrastructure projects “by administration” only when the
municipality can furnish the required tools and materials to the pakyaw
group;
b. Limit the pakyaw contracts to labor components of the infrastructure
projects; and
c. Ensure that pakyaw contracting system is supported by documents
showing the appointment of a Project Facilitator and evaluation of the
proposals of competing pakyaw groups.

11.6. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Local
Development Council, with the assistance of the Municipal Planning and
Development Office, Municipal Engineering Office, and BAC to:
a. Identify promptly the development projects to be funded under the 20%
Development Fund;
b. Ensure that detailed engineering activities, including program of work
preparation and project site acquisition, are completed thoroughly to
avoid implementation delays;
c. Review rigorously the AIP to ensure that development projects are
procurement and implementation ready; and
d. Evaluate the viability of the unimplemented development projects for
potential realignment.

13.5. We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Demand liquidation in writing from the former SB member for the
outstanding cash advances amounting to ₱13,700.00. Otherwise,
withhold any claims due to him until the cash advance is fully
liquidated; and
b. Ensure that cash advances are settled within the liquidation period.
Else, consider imposing sanctions to accountable officers who do not
liquidate their cash advances on time.

14.6. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Inventory
Committee to:
a. Update the RPCPPE to include all the insurable properties;
b. Arrange for the appraisal of all insurable properties;
c. Prepare and submit the PIF to the audit team and GSIS, not later than
April 30 of each year; and
d. Ensure the insurance of all insurable properties to protect the
government against damage or loss cause by fortuitous events.

15.7. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to reconstitute the
GFPS following the prescribed structure and composition and create a
GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team.

15.8. We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the newly
reconstituted GFPS undergo capacity building programs on GAD and ensure
that GAD Accomplishment Report is accompanied by the required
supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: 1.7. We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to record the checks as accounts payable as prescribed in Section 59 of the MNGAS.

2.10. We recommended and Management agreed to enforce the immediate settlement/liquidation of the outstanding cash advance, otherwise, demand for the refund of the entire amount.

2.11. We further recommended and the MAccO agreed to recognize the liquidation of cash advances only upon submission of complete documentation.

3.15 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. submit the SSMI to support the recognition of expenses pertaining to the issued supplies and materials for cy 2021;
b. furnish the Audit Team with the distribution lists for the rice and wheelchairs distributed to intended beneficiaries; and
c. for the foregoing years, observe the perpetual inventory method to facilitate reconciliation of records and proper presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

4.10. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Inventory and Disposal Committee to:
a. Ensure that a detailed and complete RPCPPE is prepared as a result of the physical count of PPEs to facilitate proper reconciliation of balances; and
b. Furnish the Municipal Accounting Office with relevant information and documents on the existing and turned-over/donated PPEs for updating of the accounting records and for preparation of the necessary adjusting entries.

5.15 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer agreed to secure from the Municipal Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road network.

5.16 We also recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to, thereafter, prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Report on Local Road Network in the prescribed format;

5.17 We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare and maintain the Local Network Ledger Card for the road and its components; and
b. Provide depreciation by road component after determination of the breakdown of the road components and maintenance of the necessary subsidiary ledgers, taking into consideration the guidelines set in Part V of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

6.17. We recommended and Management agreed to submit the lacking documents to establish the propriety of the procurement process.

6.18. We further recommended that Management:

a. Direct the Municipal Engineer and the Project-in-Charge to strictly monitor the status of infrastructure projects; and
b. Strictly impose liquidated damages on delays incurred by contractors.

7.9. We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly utilize Quick Relief Funds for eligible expenditures as prescribed in RA No. 10121 and JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure availability of funds in case of emergencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer secure from the Municipal Engineer and the Provincial Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant: a. prepare and maintain the Local Network Ledger Card for the road networks of the LGU; b. prepare and submit the lapsing schedule for the computation of lapsing schedules on the road network accounts; c. disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements; d. provide depreciation by road component after determination of the breakdown of the road components and maintenance of the necessary subsidiary ledgers, taking into consideration the guidelines set in Part V of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and e. draw adjusting entries to reflect the correct values of the Road Network and its corresponding accumulated depreciation account.

The total road networks was disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.
The MAccO has maintained balances in the SLs being maintained covering the entire costs of the road networks and established individual SL’s for each road network.

observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended that:
1.11.1 the Management constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete
physical count of the inventories and prepare the resulting RPCI every
semester;
1.11.2 the Municipal Treasurer GSO-Designate to maintain stock cards for all types
of inventory; and
1.11.3 the Municipal Accountant to observe the perpetual inventory method to
facilitate reconciliation of records and proper presentation of the accounts in
the financial statements.

2.4 We recommended that the Management enjoin the following officers:
2.4.1 Municipal Treasurer – GSO Designate to:
a. prepare property cards per category of PPE and maintain the same as the
property records; and
b. investigate the causes of the unaccounted PPEs amounting to ₱4,582,665.70
and determine the officers or employees accountable for the missing PPEs
based on the PARs, PCs, and any other available property records.

the assets;

accounts and their corresponding Accumulated Depreciation accounts.

a. dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures
prescribed in Section 79 of PD No 1445; and

type and other relevant information as to condition and whereabouts, as
prescribed by Annex 49, Volume II of the MNGAS.

secure from the Municipal Engineer and the Provincial Engineer the complete
description and cost segregation of road components of every road project.

components.

components;

into consideration the guidelines set in Part V of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and

corresponding Accumulated Depreciation account.

management agreed to refrain
from granting special cash
advances to elective officials,
except for their traveling
expenses, pursuant to Section
4.1.5 of COA Circular No. 97-
002.
observations.managementAction:
Special cash advances were
still granted to elective
officials. Audit Team will
continuously monitor the
actions to be undertaken
by management to resolve
the issue.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and
management agreed to furnish
the Audit Team with copies of
Memorandum of Agreement
(MOA)/Trust Agreement for all
funds received from National
Government Agencies (NGAs).
observations.managementAction:
Management to furnish to the
Audit Team, copies of
Memorandum of Agreement/
Trust Agreement for all funds
received from National
Government Agencies this CY
2017.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and
management agreed to require
all the concerned officials to
comply strictly with the
provisions of COA Circular No.
2009-001 and see to it that all
the necessary documents are
submitted to the Auditor within
the prescribed period for timely
review and verification of
transactions.
observations.managementAction:
Recommendation to be
considered this CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management reconstitute its
GFPS and redefine its functions
in accordance with the
provisions of JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction:
Management has not yet
reconstituted its GFPS. It will
study the GPFS and the JMC
No. 2013-01, and implement it
accordingly this year 2017.
observations.reason:
This was overlooked by
management.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to
management the following:
 During the preparation of its
GAD Accomplishment
Report, administer the
HGDG test on its GAD
PPAs which will serve as
basis in determining the
actual expenditure that can
be attributed to the GAD
budget pursuant to Item
4.1.4.5.e of JMC No. 2013-
01;
 Administer the HGDG test
on its PPAs for CY 2015
and onwards, the results of
which shall be the basis in
estimating the cost of
gender mainstreaming and
the GAD budget.
observations.managementAction:
The HGDG is still being
studied by management.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the
Local Sanggunian, through the
Committee on Women and
Family, shall spearhead the
formulation of the GAD Code in
collaboration with the LGU
offices/departments of the
municipal government, national
government agencies with local
presence, women’s organizations
and other civil society groups
pursuant to Item 3.2 of PCW
Memorandum Circular No.
2009-1.
observations.managementAction:
This is still to be brought out to
the new members of the
Sangguniang Bayan. The
formulation of GAD Code was
referred to the former GAD
committee however, no
Municipal GAD Code was
passed by the Municipal
Council.
observations.reason:
Management has not gathered
the required data for the
formulation of the GAD Code.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management create its GAD
M&E Team or expand the
functions and membership of the
Municipal Project Monitoring
Committee to include the GFPS
TWG Chair and representatives
from women’s organizations and
the academe pursuant to Item 5
of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction:
As of CY 2015, no GAD M&E
was created by the
municipality. Its Project
Monitoring Committee was
created under Executive Order
No. 07, S-2010, as amended by
Executive Order No. 23, S-2013
was not reconstituted to include
representatives from women’s
organizations and the
academe. The Monitoring is
being done by the Municipal
Social Welfare and Development
Officer. To be re-studied and
reconstituted this year.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the
Municipality:
 Allocate at least 5 percent of
their total budget and to
utilize the same to implement
programs and activities for
GAD concerns in accordance
with EO 273 and PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular No.
2013-01; and
 Submit the GAD Plan of the
municipality to the PPDO of
the Provincial Government
and to the DILG Regional
Office for review and
approval.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD Budget and Source of
Budget were not provided in the
prepared GAD Plan and Budget
thus, compliance on the
allocation of at least 5% of their
total budget can not be
verified.
The GAD Plan was prepared
but not submitted to the
concerned offices for review
and approval. Management
prepared a GAD Plan but was
not submitted to PPDO and
DILG for review and approval.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management, prepare a report,
which shall be approved and
submitted by the Municipal
Chief Executive to the GPPB in
printed and electronic format
within 14 days after the end of
each semester in accordance
with Section 12.2 of the Revised
IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction:
The preparation of the
Monitoring Report and the
submission of the same to the
GPPB by the BAC were not
done in CY 2016. However,
they promised to comply with
the recommendation this CY
2017.
observations.reason:
Management claimed that non-implementation is due
to work overload.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
 Require the Supply Officer to submit to the Accountant the
Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) duly
supported with the distribution lists and acknowledgement
receipts as basis of the latter in crediting in the books of
accounts inventory items issued from stock in accordance with
Section 43 and 62 of the NGAS Manual.
 Require the Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to
adjust the inventory account after considering all purchases of
inventory items during the year and coordinating with the
Supply Officer for all issuances therefrom; and
 Thereafter, all purchases should be taken up first as inventory
and succeeding issuances should be recorded as expenses based
on the RSMI prepared by the Supply Officer.

2.9 We recommended that Municipal officials and employees exercise
prudence in government spending. Reimbursement of actual
expenditures shall be allowed only if absolutely necessary with
justification and duly authorized by approving officials. We further
recommended that Management strengthen their internal control on
claims for traveling expenses.

2.9 We recommended and Management agreed to transfer the service
fees derived from Philhealth to General Fund and make the necessary
adjusting journal entries to record the amount as Miscellaneous
Income.

4.5 We recommended that the Municipal Officials and Employees
concerned refund the total amount received as incentives from the
service fees derived from Philhealth.

5.9 We recommended and Management agreed to fast-track the
implementation of duly funded priority projects in CY 2016 including
those projects not implemented in prior years and henceforth,
implement within schedule all priority projects identified in the
Annual Development Plan.

6.6 We recommended that Management appropriate the LDRRMF for
projects and activities in accordance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG
JMC No. 2013-1.

7.6 We recommended that Management appropriate the SNW to fully
attain the purpose of the funds and the benefits that could be derived
therefrom. We also recommended management to re-appropriate in
CY 2017 budget, the unappropriated balance in prior years totaling
P9,161,963.72.

8.5 We recommended and Management agreed to determine the total tax
to be withheld from the Municipal Health Personnel who received
additional compensation and collect the under withheld tax and remit
the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue. Henceforth, ensure that
taxes on additional compensation of all employees be subjected to
appropriate individual income tax rate.

9.5 We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the
amendment of the SWMP in accordance with RA No. 9003 essential
for its immediate approval and ensure an effective implementation of
the activities relative to solid waste management.

10.4 We recommended that Management assign immediately garbage
collectors to collect wastes that could be recycled and composted in
compliance to Section 10 of RA No. 9003.

11.7 We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to determine
the percentage of accomplishment on the contract expiry date and
compute for the liquidated damages to be imposed on the delinquent
contractors.

12.7 We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare and
Development Officer and the Municipal Treasurer ensure prompt
release of the social pension of senior citizen-beneficiaries which is to
be given on the first month of every quarter.

We have recommended to
management the following
remedial measures:
Copies of the Invoice
 The Municipal Rural Health
Unit (MRHU) to provide the
Municipal Accounting Office copies of the Invoice
Receipts for Property so that
the drugs and medicines,
medical, dental and laboratory
supplies, vaccines, other
supplies and PPE received
from DOH will be recognized in the
books of the Municipality;
 The MRHU to prepare a
report on the utilization of
the commodities received
from DOH-CAR duly
supported with distribution
lists and acknowledgement
receipts to be forwarded to
the Municipal Accountant
as his basis in recognizing
inventory items issued in the
books of accounts, copy
furnished the Audit Teams
of the Municipality of
Mankayan and DOH-CAR.
observations.managementAction:
Copies of the Invoice Receipts were already
provided by the MRHU to the
Municipal Accounting Office. Drugs and medicines,
vaccines, other supplies and PPE received
from DOH were already
recognized in the books under
unnumbered JEV dated
12/31/2016.
Adjustments were already
made under unnumbered JEV
dated 12/31/2016 on the
inventory items that were
issued.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and
management agreed to effect the
following journal entry to
present fairly the affected
accounts in the financial
statements:
Office Equipment 58,250.00
ICT Equipment 65,890.12
Furniture & Fixtures 7,250.00
Books 8,400.00
Prior Years Adjustments
139,790.12
observations.managementAction:
The PPE items that were erroneously recorded as office
expenses were already
adjusted and recognized in the
books in January 3, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management secure a written
authority and acquiescence of
the Office of the Solicitor
General (OSG) and written
concurrence of COA, otherwise,
the disbursements totaling
P219,889.00 and any subsequent
related disbursements shall be
disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction:
Management requested for
written authority and
concurrence for the engagement
of the services of private
counsel from the Office of the
Solicitor General. They will
write a letter to the COA
Chairman regarding the
matter. However, the OSG
stated in their reply that they
could not grant the requested
written authority and
concurrence for the
municipality to hire a legal
consultant since according to
them the OSG is not the legal
counsel of LGUs and its
officials.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management employ public
bidding as the primary mode of
procurement in compliance with
RA No. 9184 and resort to
alternative modes of
procurement only under
circumstances enumerated in the
R.A. IRR-A of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction:
Most of the purchases made
through shopping were those
equipment and office supplies
not available at the
Department of Budget and
Management-Procurement
Service and those which were
emergency in nature.
Management justified that
the needs for medicines
for the newly converted
infirmary could not be
estimated thus, they
resorted to emergency
purchases. According to
them the medicines were
purchased depending on
the number or needs of the
patients.
observations.reason:
There were still instances where public bidding was
not employed even if the
procurement does not fall
under the circumstances
enumerated in Sec. 48.2 of
R.A. IRR-A of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and
management agreed to adhere to
the provisions of Section 22 of
the Omnibus Rules on Leave and
CSC Resolution No. 00034 in
the grant of monetization of
leave credits.
observations.managementAction:
Only vacation leave credits
were allowed to be monetized
in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management submit the required
documents for the payment of
honoraria to officers/employees
concerned and ensure that
payment of honoraria to the
BAC, its Secretariat and TWG is
supported with complete
documentation.
observations.managementAction:
Requirements for the payment
of honoraria to BAC, its
Secretariat and TWG were
already submitted and
subsequent payments were
already supported with
complete documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management require the
following:
 The Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management
Officer (LDRRMO) to
submit to the COA Auditor the
Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF on or
before the 15th day after the
end of each month, and
 Municipal Accountant to
transfer to the Special Trust
Fund under the account
"Trust Liabilities-DRRM" in
the Trust Fund books the
unexpended/unobligated
balance of the QRF and
DRRMF-MOOE for CY 2014
and prior years.
Subsidiary ledgers shall be
maintained for transfers of
agency’s unutilized
DRRMF to the special trust
fund by year of transfer,
receipts of NDRRMF by
transferring agency,
DRRMF from other LGU and
other sources by donor, if any.
observations.managementAction:
This had been prepared monthly and being posted in
the required posting area of
the Municipality, copy
furnished the DILG. A report on the Sources
and Utilization of DRRMF
covering the whole year is
prepared and submitted to the
COA only when requested by
the Audit Team.
The account “Trust Liabilities -
DRRM” had been created in
the Trust Fund books for the
transfer of unexpended/unobligated
balance of DRRMF.
observations.reason:
A report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
covering the whole year is
prepared and submitted to the
COA only when requested by
the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the
Municipal Accountant agreed to
maintain a separate subsidiary
ledger for BUB funds received
from each National Government
Agency for proper accounting
control and in compliance with
COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction:
Subsidiary Ledgers were
maintained for funds received
from each National
Government Agency.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management observe the proper
matching of expenses against
appropriations pursuant to the
above-cited regulations and stop
the practice of charging prior
years expenditures to current
year appropriations. In cases
where expenses during the year
end cannot be paid, the
obligations shall be recorded as
accounts payable to validate
their payment in the succeeding
year.
observations.managementAction:
Prior year expenditures were
not charged to the current
appropriations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management comply with the
preparation of the ten-year Solid
Waste Management Plan, which
shall be reviewed and updated
every year by the municipal
management board to ensure the
efficient management of solid
waste generated within its
jurisdiction.
observations.managementAction:
Management drafted the ten-year
Solid Waste Management Plan
subject to approval by the
Regional Environment
Management Bureau (EMB).
observations.reason:
The submitted ten-year SWMP which was
submitted to the EMB for
approval was returned for
refinement to include all the
components cited in Section 17
of RA No. 9003.
(Reiterated in Part II of this
report)

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management direct the officials
and employees concerned to
refund the unauthorized excess
payment of the PEI given to
them.

The PEIs of former councilors
were deducted from the

Employees pending the

RO. Refund of PEIs of other

be implemented this CY
2017.

management require the
concerned personnel to refund
the unauthorized payment of PEI

comply with DBM Circular No.
2014-3 and other issuances on
the benefits.

Taxpayer’s index cards and

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to
management the following
measures:
 Create a Committee to
immediately conduct the
physical count of PPE and
to reconcile the results of
the physical count with the
accounting records to
ascertain the accuracy and
existence of the reported
balances in the financial
statements;
 Comply with Section 124 of
the NGAS Manual for
LGUs, Volume I on the
preparation and submission
of the Report on the
Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment
We recommended that the
Management dispose of the
unserviceable property and
equipment in accordance with
prescribed procedures.
observations.managementAction:
Each office is still reconciling
their physical count with the
accounting office.
Reconciliation of physical

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management include in its GAD
implementation report only those
activities that were included in
the Annual Performance Budget
(APB) and Annual Investment
Plan (AIP) of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
DSWD regional office programs
are no longer included as GAD
accomplishment. Only those
activities that were included in
the APB and AIP were
considered in the GAD
accomplishment report.

We have recommended that the
SB members refund the excess
RATA received.
observations.managementAction:
Refund was made by two
Sangguniang Bayan members.
Still waiting for the result of
management’s appeal to the

3, 2015 was forwarded to the
COA-CAR Regional Office.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management require officers and
employees to:
a. Comply with the rules and
regulations on the grant and
utilization of cash advances to
avoid accumulation of
unliquidated cash advances; and
b. Refund immediately the
excess amount of their cash
advances to the Collecting
Officer who shall issue the
corresponding Official
Receipt.
observations.managementAction:
Cash advances are no longer
granted to those who have
unliquidated cash advances.
Excess cash advances were
refunded. The balance of
P6,000.00 will be collected
this CY 2017.
Employees were allowed to
refund excess cash advances on
installment basis instead of
requiring immediate full
settlement.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the
Municipality limit the incurrence
of expenditures that partake of
the nature of discretionary fund
expenses to 2 percent of actual
receipts derived from basic real
property in the next preceding
calendar year, as provided under
Section 325(h) of the Local
Government Code.
We further recommended that
the Finance Committee monitor
that discretionary fund
appropriations, expenditures
incurred and payments are
within the limitation prescribed
by law.
observations.managementAction:
The incurrence of discretionary
expenses is now limited to 2
percent of actual receipts derived
from basic real property in the
next preceding calendar year.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the
Chairman of the Bids and
Awards Committee be given the
responsibility to submit copies
of all contracts together with
copies of documents as required
in Section 3.1.2 of the COA
Circular 2009-001 to the Office
of the Auditor regardless of
amount within five days upon
perfection thereof.
observations.managementAction:
Some Contracts/Purchase Orders
are submitted to the COA within

as required by the rules and
regulations. However, contracts
for major infrastructure projects
were still not submitted, instead,
these were attached to the DVs
for the claims.
